approach: API-first, cloud-native architecture on AWS Brazil South. Phase 1 delivers the WhatsApp Virtual Assistant as a standalone service with minimal core banking integration. Subsequent phases layer in KYC IDP and regulatory reporting automation, progressively integrating with core systems via an API gateway pattern.

components.name: WhatsApp Gateway
components.description: Twilio WhatsApp Business API → API Gateway → LLM Orchestration Layer
components.technology: Existing Twilio contract + AWS API Gateway

components.name: LLM Orchestration Layer
components.description: LangGraph-based stateful conversation manager. Handles intent classification, context retention across turns, and escalation routing.

components.name: RAG Knowledge Base
components.description: Vector store containing bank's product catalog, FAQ, and policy documents. Retrieval-augmented generation ensures responses are grounded in bank-specific content.

components.name: Human Escalation Router
components.description: Confidence-threshold-based escalation to human agents. Full conversation context passed to agent on handoff.

components.name: KYC Document Intelligence
components.description: Multi-modal document extraction pipeline for CPF, CNPJ, income statements, and proof of address. Human-in-the-loop review for low-confidence extractions.
components.technology: OpenAI GPT-5.4 Vision + AWS Textract (fallback) + custom validation layer

components.name: Audit & Observability Layer
components.description: Full request/response logging for every AI interaction. Required for BACEN compliance and internal model risk management.
components.technology: AWS CloudWatch + LangSmith + custom audit store (S3 + DynamoDB)

technology_choices.choice: OpenAI GPT-5.4 via Azure Brazil South
technology_choices.rationale: Data residency compliance with BACEN requirements. Same API, same models, Brazilian infrastructure.
Stateful graph-based conversation management with built-in persistence. Production-proven for banking virtual assistants.
Runs on AWS ECS (no external service). Supports incremental document updates as bank policies change.
End-to-end LLM call tracing with token-level audit. Satisfies model risk management requirements.

phases.name: WhatsApp AI Assistant — MVP
deliverables: WhatsApp virtual assistant handling top 5 customer intents
RAG knowledge base with product catalog and FAQ
Human escalation with full context handoff
Audit logging for all AI interactions
Pilot with 10,000 customers in SP region
phases.success_criteria: 50%+ deflection rate, CSAT ≥ 4.2/5.0 on AI-handled contacts

phases.name: KYC Document Intelligence
deliverables: Integration with core banking onboarding system via API
phases.success_criteria: KYC SLA reduced to 3 days, 85%+ straight-through processing rate

phases.name: Regulatory Reporting Automation
deliverables: Automated BACEN report generation from Snowflake data
Exception flagging and human review workflow
Full audit trail with source traceability
phases.success_criteria: BACEN reporting effort reduced from 3 weeks to 3 days per quarter

risks: Core banking API availability — phase 2 depends on APIs that may not exist yet
WhatsApp volume spike handling — stress test infrastructure before full rollout
Knowledge base maintenance — plan for quarterly review cycle of RAG content
review.final_recommendation: Architecture is sound and appropriately phased. Recommend starting phase 1 immediately with a 4-week scoping sprint to validate WhatsApp intent volume and core banking API readiness before committing to phase 2 timeline.
